rationale: Reader reaction to Tian Xiwei's Monte Carlo campaign look is explicitly mixed (some say it doesn't suit her, others praise boldness), no net polarity.
Harper's Bazaar Vietnam editorial frames the campaign transformation favorably as versatility, but it is still activation coverage so magnitude capped at 0.5.
Descriptive cover-story write-up of TIMETHAI wearing Generation Gucci looks; no independent critical verdict, purely activation description.
Official venue account promotional post about ambassador appearance at store event; brand/venue-owned promo copy is not public opinion.
Fan-account reshare of ambassador event footage described as fandom amplification rather than independent commentary; damped per fandom-spike trap.
Genuine collector engagement on Flora-print pieces with quantified demand signal (white GG Marmont sold out), independent judgment outside brand messaging.
Long-running contrarian-opinions thread with ongoing, split debate over whether Demna's design direction improves or dilutes the house; no clear net polarity.
